title: Brain Networks Responsible for Sense of Agency: An EEG Study.

assets.abstract: BACKGROUND: Self-agency (SA) is a person's feeling that his action was generated by himself. The neural substrates of SA have been investigated in many neuroimaging studies, but the functional connectivity of identified regions has rarely been investigated. The goal of this study is to investigate the neural network related to SA. METHODS: SA of hand movements was modulated with virtual reality. We examined the cortical network relating to SA modulation with electroencephalography (EEG) power sp

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.
